input.background: Eight diabetic patients had plasma glucose levels (mmol/l) measured before and one hour after oral administration of 100 g glucose, with the following results: Patient, Before, After, Change.
input.question: On the basis of these data, how many diabetic patients would need to be studied so that the width of the 95% confidence interval for the mean change in plasma glucose level was 0.5 mmol/l? (Assume that the Normal distribution is the appropriate sampling distribution for the change in plasma glucose.)

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: The 95% CI for the mean is the range from mean -1.96 SE to mean +1.96 SE, so the width of the CI is 2 × 1.96 × SE. For this to be equal to 0.5 mmol/l we need SE = 0.5 / (2 × 1.96) = 0.1276. Assuming that we still have SD = 2.3629, then we would have 2.3629 / sqrt(N) = 0.1276 which gives N = (2.3629 / 0.1276)^2 = 343.
